currentStudents.name: Felix Krones
currentStudents.shortBio: Felix is a DPhil student at the OII with an MSc in Industrial Engineering and Management, and has over 2 years of work experience as analytics consultant at McKinsey & Company.
currentStudents.longBio: Felix is a DPhil student at the OII with an MSc in Industrial Engineering and Management, and has over 2 years of work experience as analytics consultant at McKinsey & Company.

currentStudents.name: Djavan De Clercq
currentStudents.shortBio:
Djavan is a part-time DPhil student conducting research at the intersection of agriculture and climatic change. He is currently a consultant at McKinsey & Company. Djavan has experience in engineering, data science, venture capital, consulting, teaching, and entrepreneurship, in China, Europe, and the United States.
Djavan holds a PhD and an MSc from Tsinghua University in Environmental Science & Engineering, an MEng from UC Berkeley in Industrial Engineering & Operations Research, and a BA from Sciences Po Paris. He has published in journals such as Applied Energy, Renewable & Sustainable Energy Reviews, and Bioresource Technology.
currentStudents.longBio:
Djavan is a part-time DPhil student conducting research at the intersection of agriculture and climatic change. He is currently a consultant at McKinsey & Company. Djavan has experience in engineering, data science, venture capital, consulting, teaching, and entrepreneurship, in China, Europe, and the United States.
Djavan holds a PhD and an MSc from Tsinghua University in Environmental Science & Engineering, an MEng from UC Berkeley in Industrial Engineering & Operations Research, and a BA from Sciences Po Paris. He has published in journals such as Applied Energy, Renewable & Sustainable Energy Reviews, and Bioresource Technology.

currentStudents.name: Andrew M. Bean
currentStudents.shortBio:
Andrew is a DPhil student in Social Data Science at the OII. His research focuses on cooperative artificial intelligence. He studies potential types of human-AI cooperation, the nature of those interactions, and necessary developments to enable that cooperation. On the technical side, he has studied applied machine learning, especially reinforcement learning and natural language processing, for their applications to developing AI.
Andrew holds a B.S. in Applied Mathematics from Yale University and an MSc in Social Data Science from the OII. He is a Clarendon Scholar and was previously a Thouron Prize winner at the University of Cambridge (Pembroke College).
currentStudents.longBio:
Andrew is a DPhil student in Social Data Science at the OII. His research focuses on cooperative artificial intelligence. He studies potential types of human-AI cooperation, the nature of those interactions, and necessary developments to enable that cooperation. On the technical side, he has studied applied machine learning, especially reinforcement learning and natural language processing, for their applications to developing AI.
Andrew holds a B.S. in Applied Mathematics from Yale University and an MSc in Social Data Science from the OII. He is a Clarendon Scholar and was previously a Thouron Prize winner at the University of Cambridge (Pembroke College).

currentStudents.name: Yushi Yang
currentStudents.shortBio: Yushi is a DPhil student in Social Data Science at the OII. Her research focuses on LLM Post-training, Agents, and AI Alignment.
currentStudents.longBio:
Yushi is a DPhil student in Social Data Science at the OII. Her research focuses on LLM post-training, agents, and AI alignment.
Yushi holds an MSc in Artificial Intelligence from Imperial College London and an MSc in Statistical Science from the University of Oxford. She also worked as an AI engineer at Reply and a data analyst at UNDP.
Her skillsets include Python, PyTorch, SQL, and AWS services.

currentStudents.name: Harry Mayne
currentStudents.focus: LLM Interpretability, AI Safety, LLM Evaluations
currentStudents.shortBio: Harry's DPhil focuses on language model explainability and interpretability. His current research explores whether models can reliably explain their outputs in natural language, and what the implications are for broader human-computer interaction. Harry has previously worked on mechanistic interpretability problems and continues to contribute to this area.
currentStudents.longBio: Harry's DPhil focuses on language model explainability and interpretability. His current research explores whether models can reliably explain their outputs in natural language, and what the implications are for broader human-computer interaction. Harry has previously worked on mechanistic interpretability problems and continues to contribute to this area.

currentStudents.name: Jessica Rodrigues
currentStudents.shortBio: Jessica is a DPhil student in Social Data Science, researching the societal impact of research using NLP and knowledge graphs. She holds an MSc in Computer Science and works as a Lead Data Scientist.
currentStudents.longBio:
Jessica is studying part-time for a DPhil in Social Data Science. Her research lies at the intersection of Natural Language Processing (NLP), Scientific AI, and Metascience. Through her project Flowmetrics, she proposes a new generation of research metrics that conceptualise societal impact as a dynamic semantic flow—traced and modelled using large language models and knowledge graphs.
Flowmetrics builds on the lineage of bibliometrics, scientometrics, webometrics, and altmetrics, but for the first time introduces AI-driven techniques to infer how research disseminates, engages audiences, and produces real-world outcomes. Using NLP methods and structured ontologies, the project extracts and classifies signals of impact across platforms and represents them as evolving trajectories within a knowledge graph.
Alongside her research, Jessica works full-time for Springer Nature as a Lead Data Scientist, where she develops machine learning systems for peer review, research integrity, and editorial decision support. She has also worked across a range of industries in data science roles and holds an MSc in Computer Science with a research focus on Machine Learning and NLP.

currentStudents.name: Guy Parsons
currentStudents.shortBio: Guy is an academic clinician in Intensive Care and previously the Clinical Lead of a Health AI products business at Deloitte. He currently works across LMIC settings developing digital health ecosystems with Governments/NGOs/private-public partnerships. He has considerable experience in bringing AI-based Software-as-a-Medical-Device products to market and the associated regulation and governance involved. He holds an MBBS (Clinical Medicine), BSc (Medical Sciences), and Masters degree (History of Medicine) from University College London, an MBA and multiple postgraduate medical qualifications including specialist training in clinical safety for novel technologies. He is interested in the development of AI-enabled healthcare systems, their human-AI interactions, and their trustworthiness, governance, and equity.
currentStudents.longBio: Guy is an academic clinician in Intensive Care and previously the Clinical Lead of a Health AI products business at Deloitte. He currently works across LMIC settings developing digital health ecosystems with Governments/NGOs/private-public partnerships. He has considerable experience in bringing AI-based Software-as-a-Medical-Device products to market and the associated regulation and governance involved. He holds an MBBS (Clinical Medicine), BSc (Medical Sciences), and Masters degree (History of Medicine) from University College London, an MBA and multiple postgraduate medical qualifications including specialist training in clinical safety for novel technologies. He is interested in the development of AI-enabled healthcare systems, their human-AI interactions, and their trustworthiness, governance, and equity.

currentStudents.name: Karolina Korgul
currentStudents.shortBio: Karolina is a DPhil student in Social Data Science and a Clarendon Scholar at the OII. Her research focuses on developing GenAI-based solution for the healthcare field and evaluating the quality of current implementations. She is also particularly interested in how AI development shapes the global labour market and human behaviour, collaborating with the Polish Economic Institute to evaluate its impact on these areas.
currentStudents.longBio:
Karolina is a DPhil student in Social Data Science and a Clarendon Scholar at the OII. Her research focuses on developing GenAI-based solution for the healthcare field and evaluating the quality of current implementations. She is also particularly interested in how AI development shapes the global labour market and human behaviour, collaborating with the Polish Economic Institute to evaluate its impact on these areas.
Alongside her doctoral research, Karolina works as a Technical Account Manager at Google Cloud, specialising in GenAI solutions.
Karolina holds degrees from the University of Oxford (MSc) and the University of Warsaw (BSc), with an exchange stay at the University of Lisbon.

currentStudents.name: Ryan Othniel Kearns
currentStudents.focus: Science of Evals, Reasoning in LLMs
currentStudents.shortBio: Ryan Othniel Kearns is a DPhil student in the Reasoning with Machines Lab, generously supported by the Clarendon Scholarship. His research focuses on understanding reasoning in large language models (LLMs) and the science of AI evaluations. Previously, Ryan studied Philosophy (BAH) and Computer Science (BS) at Stanford University and Social Data Science (MSc) at the University of Oxford.
currentStudents.longBio:
Ryan Othniel Kearns is a DPhil student in the Reasoning with Machines Lab and a research fellow with the Oxford Human-Centered AI Lab. His research focuses on understanding reasoning in large language models (LLMs) and the science of AI evaluations. Previously, Ryan studied Philosophy (BAH) and Computer Science (BS) at Stanford University and Social Data Science (MSc) at the University of Oxford.
Ryan's research is supported by a Clarendon Scholarship and a Cosmos Fellowship.

currentStudents.name: Shreyansh Padarha
currentStudents.focus: AI for Science, AI Safety, LLM Evaluations
currentStudents.shortBio:
Shreyansh is a Research Assistant at the Reasoning with Machines Lab and an incoming DPhil student in Social Data Science, supported by the ESRC Grand Union DTP studentship. He previously studied Social Data Science (MSc) at the University of Oxford. His research motivation is to push the performative boundaries of AI models while stress-testing their safety.
His current work focuses on LLMs and agents that automate scientific processes and building faithful evaluations.
currentStudents.longBio:
Shreyansh is a Research Assistant at the Reasoning with Machines Lab and an incoming (2026) DPhil student in Social Data Science, supported by the ESRC Grand Union DTP studentship.
His research is motivated by the goal of pushing the performative boundaries of AI models while stress-testing their safety. His current work focuses on LLMs and agents that automate scientific processes, as well as building faithful evaluations for knowledge synthesis.
He previously studied Social Data Science (MSc) at the University of Oxford, focusing on emergent misalignment in knowledge distillation. Prior to this, during his undergraduate studies, he worked at startups and as a researcher on projects involving building datasets and training computer vision models for agriculture, dentistry, and the entertainment industry.

currentStudents.name: Mia Kussman
currentStudents.shortBio: Mia is an MSc Student at the Reasoning with Machines Lab, studying Social Data Science at the University of Oxford. Her current research focuses on evaluating the socio-communicative abilities of LLMs as medical assistants for the general public.
currentStudents.longBio: Mia is an MSc student in the Reasoning with Machines Lab, studying Social Data Science at the University of Oxford. Her current research focuses on evaluating the socio-communicative abilities of LLMs as medical assistants for the general public. Prior to this, she studied psychology and linguistics at Haverford College, and worked as a researcher in the Social Neuroscience Lab at Princeton University, where she investigated emotional conversations between humans.

currentStudents.name: Caleb Tan
currentStudents.shortBio: Caleb Tan is a MSc Social Data Science Student in the Reasoning with Machines Lab and the Digital Ethics and Defence Technologies Research Group at the Oxford Internet Institute.
currentStudents.longBio: Caleb Tan is a MSc Social Data Science Student in the Reasoning with Machines Lab and the Digital Ethics and Defence Technologies Research Group at the Oxford Internet Institute. His research focuses on the reasoning of LLMs in high-risk domains, and is supervised by Professor Mariarosaria Taddeo and Professor Adam Mahdi. He holds a BSc (Econ) in Economics and Geography from University College London.

currentStudents.name: Amelia Mercado
currentStudents.focus: Clinical AI, Computational Psychiatry, Human-LLM Interaction
currentStudents.shortBio: Amelia is a medical student at Baylor College of Medicine who is pursuing an MSc in Social Data Science at the University of Oxford. As a future psychiatrist, her research focuses on the intersection of artificial intelligence and clinical medicine. Co-supervised by Adam Mahdi and Robert McCutcheon, she works within the OxRML and Oxford Psychosis Research & Application (OPERA) labs to apply machine learning to schizophrenia outcomes. Her broader research also investigates human-LLM interactions in mental health care and medicine.
currentStudents.longBio: Amelia is a medical student at Baylor College of Medicine and a Rotary Global Grant Scholar pursuing an MSc in Social Data Science at the University of Oxford. As a future psychiatrist, her research focuses on the intersection of artificial intelligence and clinical medicine. Under the co-supervision of Adam Mahdi and Robert McCutcheon, she works within the OxRML and Oxford Psychosis Research & Application (OPERA) labs to apply machine learning to schizophrenia outcomes. Her broader work investigates human-LLM interactions in mental health care and medicine. Previously, she earned a Bachelor of Science and Arts in Biology from the University of Texas at Austin, graduating with Highest Honors and a Distinction in Research.

currentStudents.name: Sebastian Petric
currentStudents.shortBio:
Sebastian Petric began his career in emerging market asset management, which heightened his awareness of the diverse factors influencing returns and risks. This experience guided him towards multi-asset class investing and capital market research, where he developed a passion for data science and machine learning.
As a future-focused leader and a Visiting Policy Fellow at the Oxford Internet Institute, he is equipped with extensive knowledge and expertise in innovative technologies. He strives to make a difference through his roles in advisory and advocacy, and he aims to influence through knowledge sharing via his publications.
Alongside his thought leadership, he is committed to actively contributing to and empowering communities in their efforts to address economic, social, and environmental challenges.

currentStudents.name: Tristan Naidoo
currentStudents.shortBio: Tristan is a Research Associate in AI for Health Data Systems at Imperial College London. His current research focuses on the equitable distribution and use of AI tools as means of improving public health. Within this, he has a particular interest in LLMs and agentic AI, which has led to his affiliation with the Reasoning with Machines Lab.
currentStudents.longBio:
Tristan is a Research Associate in AI for Health Data Systems at Imperial College London. His current research focuses on the equitable distribution and use of AI tools as a means of improving public health. Within this, he has a particular interest in LLMs and agentic AI, which has led to his affiliation with the Reasoning with Machines Lab.

Before his current role, Tristan completed a PhD at Imperial College London, where he used NLP to study public discourse during the COVID-19 pandemic, and an MSc in Advanced Analytics from the University of Cape Town. Between his studies, he worked at a data science start-up as both a Data Scientist and a Technical Lead.
